sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Manchester City are in strong form, remaining unbeaten in their last 7 matches with 4 wins and a draw in their last 5. Everton have shown mixed results, managing 2 wins, 2 losses, and a draw in their recent 5 games. City's attacking prowess, led by Erling Haaland (7 goals in last 5), gives them a clear edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Manchester City have dominated Everton, securing a 2-0 win in April 2025 and a 1-1 draw in December 2024. City have not lost to Everton in 17 meetings, often scoring first, which highlights their historical superiority in this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Manchester City rank 2nd in the league for goals scored (15) and have a robust defense (5th least goals conceded). Everton, positioned 8th, have scored 9 goals but struggle defensively, failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 4 matches. City's superior possession (57%) and passing accuracy (87%) further tilt the balance.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: City’s ability to create big chances (3rd in league) and their high pressing (6th in possessions won in final third) could exploit Everton’s defensive vulnerabilities. Everton’s tendency to concede cards (4/5 matches over 4.5 cards) might also play into City’s hands. Sofascore’s prediction of a 71% home win aligns with these trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the score remains level at 0-0 between Manchester City and Everton at the Etihad Stadium. Despite the lack of goals, the first half was far from uneventful, with both teams creating opportunities to break the deadlock. Manchester City dominated possession and dictated the tempo for large spells, while Everton showed resilience in defense and occasional sharpness on the counter. Neither side, however, could capitalize on their chances, with missed opportunities and solid defending keeping the game goalless.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Manchester City's dominance
halftimeSections.text: Manchester City clearly controlled the game in terms of ball possession and territorial advantage. They consistently entered the final third and created numerous opportunities in and around Everton’s penalty area. The home side earned a significant number of corner kicks and had several close calls, including hitting the woodwork once. While they missed a big chance, their attacking intent was evident, and they will likely look to convert this pressure into goals in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Everton's defensive resolve
halftimeSections.text: Everton, on the other hand, adopted a more pragmatic approach, focusing on defensive organization while seeking chances on the break. They managed to create a big chance of their own, though it went unconverted. Their aerial strength was notable, winning a higher percentage of duels in the air compared to their opponents. Despite being under pressure for much of the half, their backline held firm, supported by key clearances from players like James Tarkowski and Michael Keane.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum shifts
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw an initial surge from Everton, who started with intent and looked to unsettle Manchester City in the opening minutes. However, the momentum quickly shifted to the home side, who took control with sustained periods of attacking pressure, especially from the middle of the half onwards. City had several prolonged spells of dominance, relentlessly pushing forward. Everton had a brief resurgence late in the half with a dangerous attacking moment, but City regained control before the break, finishing the half on the front foot.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Manchester City, players like Jérémy Doku and Phil Foden stood out with their creativity and involvement in dangerous moves. Doku, in particular, had shots on target and missed a big chance, while Foden’s key passes posed constant threats. On Everton’s side, Iliman Ndiaye was a bright spark, contributing to their attacking output with a shot on target and a big chance created. Defensively, Tarkowski and Keane were pivotal in keeping City at bay with crucial interventions.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Manchester City are likely to continue their dominance and push for a breakthrough. Their attacking quality and territorial control suggest they are the favorites to open the scoring. Everton will need to remain compact and capitalize on any counter-attacking opportunities, as they did sporadically in the first half. Tactical adjustments from either side could play a crucial role, with City potentially looking to exploit set-pieces and Everton aiming to frustrate with a deep defensive line.
